title: Detecting Duplicates in a Linked List

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: A linked list with at least one node, where node data is integer
statement.outputFormat: True if there are duplicates, False otherwise
statement.notes: The implementation should handle the general case of finding duplicates in a linked list.

hints.text: Use a Set to store unique elements of the linked list

editorial.title: Detecting Duplicates in a Linked List
editorial.intuition: Iterate over the linked list, adding each node's value to a Set. If any value is already in the Set, return True.
editorial.bruteForce: Compare each node to every other node in the list to detect duplicates
editorial.betterSolution: Use a Set to keep track of unique elements
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate over the linked list once, checking each node's value against the Set
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Sets automatically eliminate duplicates, making the solution correct
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check for duplicates when iterating over the list
Using a different data structure without checking for duplicates

buggyStarterCode.python:
class Solution:

def has_duplicates(self, head):
    if head is None: return False
    seen = set()
    seen.add(head.val)
    cur = head.next
    while cur is not None:
        if cur.val in seen: return True
        seen.add(cur.val)
        cur = cur.next
    return False

solutions.python:
import sys
from typing import List

# Definition for singly-linked list.
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

class Solution:
    def has_duplicates(self, head: ListNode) -> bool:
        seen = set()
        cur = head
        while cur is not None:
            if cur.val in seen:
                return True
            seen.add(cur.val)
            cur = cur.next
        return False

if __name__ == '__main__':
    lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    n = int(lines[0])
    arr = [int(x) for x in lines[1].split()]
    arr = [0] + arr
    dummy = ListNode(0)
    cur = dummy
    for num in arr[1:]:
        cur.next = ListNode(num)
        cur = cur.next
    solver = Solution()
    print(solver.has_duplicates(dummy.next))
